scope: Add Cpp-specific arm to extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:3945) handling tree-sitter-cpp `declaration → init_declarator(value=argument_list)` shape; extend the per-language descend_kinds match arm INSIDE the OUTER extract_first_identifier_arg_ast at taint.rs L4117-4129 with a Language::Cpp branch covering binary_expression / call_expression / parenthesized_expression / argument_list (the inner descent helper extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent at L4180+ performs unconditional BFS and has NO per-language extension point; outer-helper descend_kinds extension is FORWARD-COVERAGE for future Cpp call_expression-rooted nested-constructor sinks; the new Cpp entry arm short-circuits before L4076 for cpp_deserialization_positive). Reuses existing descent helper (introduced by var-extract-nested-constructor-v1; depth 5; string-kind filter at every level). Closes vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B Cpp partial (cpp_deserialization_positive — the LAST remaining carry-forward) per var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 premortem amendment A1 (commit 88f5620). NO public API change; NO new VulnType / TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType variants; NO bank entry additions; NO push, NO publish, NO version bump (USER STANDING RULE).
closes_carry_forward: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B Cpp partial (cpp_deserialization_positive); deferred from var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 per premortem A1. THE LAST REMAINING vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forward.
expected_post_milestone_red_count_note: Drops vuln_migration_v1_red red count from 1 to 0 (167/168 GREEN → 168/168 GREEN). Closes the LAST remaining carry-forward from vuln-source-parity-v1.

validator_mandates.no_public_api_change: M2 MUST NOT change the signature of extract_first_identifier_arg_ast or extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent. New arm is internal additive logic.
validator_mandates.no_new_enum_variants: M2 MUST NOT add new VulnType, TaintSinkType, or TaintSourceType variants.
validator_mandates.no_bank_additions: M2 MUST NOT modify CPP_AST_SINKS, CPP_AST_SOURCES, CPP_AST_SANITIZERS. Bank entries already correct (boost::archive::text_iarchive at L2658, cereal::BinaryInputArchive at L2659).
validator_mandates.no_call_node_kinds_modification: M2 MUST NOT modify call_node_kinds() in ast_utils.rs. Cpp arm is internal to the helper and language-specific.
validator_mandates.no_member_patterns_match_modification: M2 MUST NOT modify member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3856). Sink-pattern matching already fires correctly.
validator_mandates.no_extract_call_name_modification: M2 MUST NOT modify extract_call_name_* per-language helpers in ast_utils.rs.
validator_mandates.additive_or_minimal_change: M2 single source file (crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs); new arm placed AFTER existing OCaml arm and BEFORE generic args lookup; per-language descend-through extension is one new match arm.
validator_mandates.m2_atomic_if_multi_file: M2 is single-file by design — atomic-commit constraint trivially satisfied.
validator_mandates.m2_string_kind_filter_preservation: Reuses extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent which applies string_node_kinds(language) filter at every recursion level — closes-#24 regression-guard preserved by construction.
validator_mandates.preserve_existing_cpp_tests_green: M3 GREEN-regression sweep: all 167 currently-GREEN tests in vuln_migration_v1_red MUST remain GREEN. Any GREEN→RED transition triggers immediate M2 revert and root-cause investigation.
validator_mandates.no_push_no_publish: USER STANDING RULE. M3 MUST NOT push to remote, MUST NOT run cargo publish, MUST NOT bump any version. Local tag only.
validator_mandates.cargo_lock_never_staged: Per-commit guard: `git checkout HEAD -- Cargo.lock` if dirty before staging.
validator_mandates.m1_red_capture_required: M1 MUST run cpp_deserialization_positive at HEAD 7a36df3 and confirm RED pre-fix. Document in reports/M1-red-capture.txt.
validator_mandates.m1_green_baseline_required: M1 MUST capture 167/168 GREEN baseline of vuln_migration_v1_red at HEAD 7a36df3. Document in reports/M1-green-baseline.txt. M3 compares against this baseline for GREEN→RED regression detection.
validator_mandates.m3_red_count_delta_assertion: M3 MUST verify vuln_migration_v1_red red count drops by exactly 1 (1 → 0; cpp_deserialization_positive closes). Any other delta triggers investigation before tag.
validator_mandates.m3_binary_smoke_string_literal_fp: M3 MUST run binary smoke (`tldr vuln <fixture>`) on all fixtures matching the glob crates/tldr-cli/tests/fixtures/vuln_migration_v1/*/deserialization_string_literal_fp.* (13 deserialization-specific fixtures; the broader *_string_literal_fp glob across the fixture tree expands to 84 total) and document 0 findings each. Glob is authoritative; the count corrected per premortem amendment A2. Closes-#24 regression-guard.
validator_mandates.m3_changelog_entry: M3 CHANGELOG entry MUST cite that NO public API change occurred and NO new TaintSinkType / TaintSourceType / VulnType variants were added; MUST cite predecessor (var-extract-nested-constructor-v1) and the premortem amendment A1 reference.

staging_method.verification: git show HEAD --stat | grep -E 'continuum/autonomous/(?!cpp-deser-declaration-v1-plan/)' && echo LEAK || echo STAGING-OK
staging_method.rationale: Prevents accidental staging of unrelated continuum/autonomous artifacts; only this plan's reports + the source code edit at M2 + CHANGELOG.md at M3 are eligible. Cargo.lock NEVER staged.

milestones.title: RED capture + investigation memo + design choice

investigations.purpose: Re-confirm tree-sitter-cpp shape and document Option A vs B vs C choice
methods: Run `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red cpp_deserialization_positive` at HEAD 7a36df3 → expect FAIL. Capture stdout/stderr to reports/M1-red-capture.txt.
Run `cargo test -p tldr-cli --release --test vuln_migration_v1_red --no-fail-fast` (full suite) → expect 167/168 GREEN. Capture summary in reports/M1-green-baseline.txt.
Re-confirm via `target/release/tldr taint <fixture> handler --format json` that source `d` is detected (env_var) and sinks=[] empirically (already established in reports/investigation.json).
Write reports/M1-disposition.json recording chosen Option A and rationale (helper-level Cpp arm; sink bank already correct).
investigations.decision_outcome: Option A — helper-level Cpp arm in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast walking declaration → init_declarator(value=argument_list) → delegate to extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent. Options B (sink-dispatch-level) and C (declaration → call_node_kinds) rejected — see plan §3.

stop_thresholds: 1 RED captured at HEAD 7a36df3 in reports/M1-red-capture.txt
167-test GREEN baseline of vuln_migration_v1_red captured in reports/M1-green-baseline.txt
reports/M1-disposition.json written with Option A choice + rationale

milestones.title: Implement Cpp arm in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast + outer-helper descend_kinds extension at L4117-4129 (forward-coverage)

green_files.anchor: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast at L3945 (entry arm) + outer-helper descend_kinds match at L4117-4129 (forward-coverage). NOTE: inner descent helper extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent at L4180+ is body-unchanged — it does unconditional BFS and has NO per-language extension point.
additions: Add new Cpp arm in extract_first_identifier_arg_ast AFTER OCaml application_expression arm (L4043) and BEFORE generic args lookup (L4076). Arm: when `language == Language::Cpp && descendant.kind() == "declaration"`, iterate named children, find init_declarator, extract child_by_field_name("value") (must be argument_list), invoke extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent(value, source, language, 0). Return None on miss so outer fallback chain still runs.

Extend the per-language descend_kinds match arm INSIDE the OUTER extract_first_identifier_arg_ast (taint.rs L4117-L4129) with a Language::Cpp arm: &["binary_expression", "call_expression", "parenthesized_expression", "argument_list"]. NOTE: this is the outer helper's descend_kinds set — the inner descent helper at L4180+ performs unconditional BFS and has no per-language extension point. This extension is FORWARD-COVERAGE for future Cpp call_expression-rooted nested-constructor sinks; the new Cpp entry arm short-circuits before L4076 for cpp_deserialization_positive, so this set is not on the critical path for the carry-forward fixture.

Add CPP-DESER-DECLARATION-V1 doc-comment block citing M1 disposition (Option A), premortem 88f5620 reference, predecessor var-extract-nested-constructor-v1, and that CPP_AST_SINKS L2655-2662 is unchanged.
preserved: All existing language-specific arms (PHP echo_statement L3954-3982, Ruby subshell L4013-4036, OCaml application_expression L4043-4070)
string_node_kinds(language) filter applied at every recursion level inside extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent
Both invocation sites at taint.rs:4201 (sources) and :4316 (sinks) — no caller-side change needed

stop_thresholds: All 167 currently-GREEN tests in vuln_migration_v1_red remain GREEN (gating)
M2 source diff confined to crates/tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs (single file)

milestones.title: Verify + binary smoke + CHANGELOG + local tag

names: full vuln_migration_v1_red sweep + workspace cargo test + binary smoke

green_files.purpose: Internal-milestone entry per var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 / ruby-backtick-extraction-v1 / rust-vuln-taint-pipeline-v1 precedent
sections: ### Changed: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast new Cpp arm for declaration → init_declarator(value=argument_list) shape; extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent extended with Language::Cpp descend-through set (binary_expression, call_expression, parenthesized_expression, argument_list).

### Closed carry-forward: cpp_deserialization_positive (vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B Cpp partial; deferred from var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 per premortem A1). vuln_migration_v1_red 167/168 GREEN → 168/168 GREEN. Closes the LAST remaining vuln-source-parity-v1 carry-forward.
### Retained: closes-#24 string-literal regression-guard preserved at every descent level; CPP_AST_SINKS bank unchanged; all currently-GREEN tests remain GREEN.
### Architectural note: NO public API change; NO new variants; NO bank additions; helper extension only. NO push, NO publish, NO version bump.

per_language_verdict._note: Single-language milestone targeting cpp_deserialization_positive specifically. No other language affected. Predecessor var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 closed Java + Scala via the same descent helper (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent); this milestone adds the Cpp entry arm + Cpp descend-through set entry.

loc_delta_summary.note: Smallest of the three follow-on milestones. Single-function entry arm + per-language descend-through set augmentation in single source file. Doc-comment block ~10-15 LOC of the 40.

investigation_findings.tree_sitter_parse: declaration → init_declarator { declarator: identifier(ia), value: argument_list { binary_expression(>>) { left: call_expression { function: qualified_identifier(std::stringstream), arguments: argument_list { identifier(d) } }, right: identifier(obj) } } }
investigation_findings.no_function_declarator: Empirically refuted in predecessor premortem (commit 88f5620). The C++ 'most-vexing-parse' direct-init declaration is parsed as init_declarator with `value` field = argument_list, NOT function_declarator.

empirical_taint_output_at_head.sources: [{var:'d', line:6, source_type:'env_var'}] — std::getenv source detected correctly
empirical_taint_output_at_head.sinks: [] — sink dispatch matches the descendant via raw-substring fallback in member_patterns_match (taint.rs:3924-3928), but var-extraction returns None across all 4 fallback paths (extract_first_identifier_arg_ast generic args lookup; extract_assignment_rhs_ident no-= line; call_kinds synthetic; assignment_rhs_ident no-=).

investigation_findings.root_cause: Var-extraction shape coverage gap: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast does not handle Cpp `declaration` shape. The generic positional fallback (taint.rs:4081-4090) scans direct children for kind containing 'argument' — declaration's direct children are qualified_identifier (type) and init_declarator — neither match. Same shape of gap as PHP echo_statement (added L3954-3982), Ruby subshell (added L4013-4036), OCaml application_expression (added L4043-4070) — all language-specific arms.
investigation_findings.fix_shape: Helper-level Cpp arm; reuses extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent (already exists at L4180; introduced by var-extract-nested-constructor-v1) with one new per-language descend-through entry (Language::Cpp).
investigation_findings.cpp_ast_sinks_status: Already correct — boost::archive::text_iarchive at L2658, cereal::BinaryInputArchive at L2659. No bank changes needed.
investigation_findings.member_patterns_match_status: Already fires raw-substring fallback (taint.rs:3924-3928) correctly. No member-match changes needed.

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: Cpp arm fires on UNRELATED `declaration` nodes (e.g., `int x = foo(d);`) and surfaces false positives across pre-existing GREEN cpp fixtures
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Arm invoked ONLY in var-extraction path which itself runs ONLY when sink-pattern already matched. Structural chain check (declaration → init_declarator with value=argument_list) is specific. M3 GREEN-regression sweep gating across all 167 currently-GREEN tests.

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent Cpp descend-through additions regress var-extraction for Java/Scala or other-Cpp call_expression sinks
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Per-language descend-through set explicitly keyed on Language::Cpp via match arm. Java/Scala/other-language descent paths unchanged. M3 sweep gating.

extract_first_identifier_arg_ast_descent ALREADY applies string_node_kinds(language) filter at every recursion level — unchanged. Cpp arm reuses that helper. M3 binary smoke on all 13 deserialization-specific (or 84 total via the broader *_string_literal_fp glob) fixtures gating; glob authoritative per premortem amendment A2.

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: Tree-sitter-cpp grammar version drift renames init_declarator or argument_list
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Helper comment cites pinned grammar; arm fails closed (returns None) on shape mismatch — no regression, fixture re-RED would surface in CI.

premortem_top_risks_summary.description: Single-constructor case (no binary_expression) wrapping the inner identifier
premortem_top_risks_summary.mitigation: Descend-through set covers call_expression directly, so single-constructor case is also handled. No additional risk.

carry_forward_after_milestone.remaining_buckets: NONE — closes the LAST remaining carry-forward from vuln-source-parity-v1.
carry_forward_after_milestone.rationale: vuln-source-parity-v1 M5 Bucket B Cpp partial (cpp_deserialization_positive) was the LAST deferred fixture across the entire vuln-migration-v1 / vuln-source-parity-v1 chain. Closure brings vuln_migration_v1_red to 168/168 GREEN.

pipeline_metadata.predecessor: var-extract-nested-constructor-v1 (locally tagged at HEAD b577796; closed Java + Scala subset of Bucket B)
